turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="#f8fbff"/>

  <!-- Plate -->
  <circle cx="256" cy="280" r="160" fill="#fffaf0" stroke="#e0e0e0" stroke-width="12"/>
  <circle cx="256" cy="280" r="120" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#f2f2f2" stroke-width="6"/>

  <!-- Fork (left) -->
  <g transform="translate(150,140) rotate(-35)">
    <rect x="0" y="0" width="18" height="170" rx="6" fill="#b0b7bd"/>
    <rect x="-6" y="-10" width="30" height="36" rx="4" fill="#b0b7bd"/>
    <rect x="0" y="-10" width="4" height="22" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.25"/>
    <rect x="6" y="-10" width="4" height="22" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.25"/>
    <rect x="12" y="-10" width="4" height="22" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.25"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Spoon (right) -->
  <g transform="translate(330,150) rotate(25)">
    <rect x="0" y="60" width="14" height="150" rx="6" fill="#b0b7bd"/>
    <ellipse cx="7" cy="46" rx="28" ry="38" fill="#dfe5e8" stroke="#b0b7bd" stroke-width="4"/>
    <ellipse cx="7" cy="38" rx="14" ry="20" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.25"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Apple with a bite -->
  <g>
    <circle cx="256" cy="240" r="48" fill="#d93b3b" stroke="#b83030" stroke-width="3"/>
    <!-- bite (overlay matching plate interior) -->
    <circle cx="286" cy="220" r="20" fill="#ffffff"/>
    <!-- subtle shine -->
    <ellipse cx="242" cy="216" rx="12" ry="8" fill="#ff6b6b" opacity="0.35"/>
    <!-- stem -->
    <rect x="250" y="188" width="6" height="18" rx="2" fill="#7b4f2a" transform="rotate(-22 253 197)"/>
    <!-- leaf -->
    <path d="M270 195 C278 184 292 182 294 172 C288 176 282 174 276 180 C270 186 268 194 270 195 Z" fill="#4ea63b"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Soft shadow under plate -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="420" rx="140" ry="28" fill="#000000" opacity="0.06"/>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.content:
{
  "svg": "<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="#f8fbff"/>

  <!-- Plate -->
  <circle cx="256" cy="280" r="160" fill="#fffaf0" stroke="#e0e0e0" stroke-width="12"/>
  <circle cx="256" cy="280" r="120" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#f2f2f2" stroke-width="6"/>

  <!-- Fork (left) -->
  <g transform="translate(150,140) rotate(-35)">
    <rect x="0" y="0" width="18" height="170" rx="6" fill="#b0b7bd"/>
    <rect x="-6" y="-10" width="30" height="36" rx="4" fill="#b0b7bd"/>
    <rect x="0" y="-10" width="4" height="22" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.25"/>
    <rect x="6" y="-10" width="4" height="22" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.25"/>
    <rect x="12" y="-10" width="4" height="22" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.25"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Spoon (right) -->
  <g transform="translate(330,150) rotate(25)">
    <rect x="0" y="60" width="14" height="150" rx="6" fill="#b0b7bd"/>
    <ellipse cx="7" cy="46" rx="28" ry="38" fill="#dfe5e8" stroke="#b0b7bd" stroke-width="4"/>
    <ellipse cx="7" cy="38" rx="14" ry="20" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.25"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Apple with a bite -->
  <g>
    <circle cx="256" cy="240" r="48" fill="#d93b3b" stroke="#b83030" stroke-width="3"/>
    <!-- bite (overlay matching plate interior) -->
    <circle cx="286" cy="220" r="20" fill="#ffffff"/>
    <!-- subtle shine -->
    <ellipse cx="242" cy="216" rx="12" ry="8" fill="#ff6b6b" opacity="0.35"/>
    <!-- stem -->
    <rect x="250" y="188" width="6" height="18" rx="2" fill="#7b4f2a" transform="rotate(-22 253 197)"/>
    <!-- leaf -->
    <path d="M270 195 C278 184 292 182 294 172 C288 176 282 174 276 180 C270 186 268 194 270 195 Z" fill="#4ea63b"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Soft shadow under plate -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="420" rx="140" ry="28" fill="#000000" opacity="0.06"/>
</svg>"
}
message.reasoning:
**Creating SVG for "eat" concept**

I need to produce a JSON object that contains an SVG string representing the idea of eating, without any text included. It should focus on simple shapes like a plate, fork, spoon, and knife, while also suggesting a person's mouth biting. An apple with a bite taken could symbolize eating effectively. The SVG must be valid, fit into a 512x512 canvas, and use path shapes to illustrate elements like a plate and food without any lettering.**Constructing SVG JSON**

I need to create a simple SVG representation for a plate, with a light gray rim, a fork on the left, a spoon on the right, and an apple with a bite on the plate. The color palette should be straightforward. I have to ensure there are no foreign objects or scripts included, and set the dimensions to 512x512. I'll structure the JSON object with the field "svg," making sure to properly escape any inner double quotes while avoiding trailing commas.**Drawing SVG Elements**

I'm crafting an SVG with a 512x512 size. First, I’ll create a white background, then draw a plate using concentric circles to give it a nice look. For the fork on the left, instead of just rectangles, I'll use paths to simplify the design. I need to select the correct coordinates to ensure the fork properly overlays the plate. There’s some fine-tuning necessary so it looks just right as I refine the shapes and their transform properties.**Positioning SVG Elements**

I'm aiming to position the plate at (256, 280) with a radius of 160. The fork will be rotated -30 degrees and positioned so the prongs are above the plate, which I can simplify by using a transformation to translate and rotate it. For the spoon, I’ll draw an ellipse for the head and handle rotated at 20 degrees. The apple will be the centerpiece, created with a red circle and a white overlay to simulate a bite, along with a green leaf and brown stem for detail.**Finalizing SVG JSON**

I think I'm ready to complete the SVG for the design. I'll add highlights to the apple with a lighter ellipse and craft the spoon by drawing its handle and oval head on the right. It’s important to ensure everything is simple and clear with no text involved. I'll correct the fill for shadows, using `fill="#000"` with opacity for older compatibility. Once everything looks good, I’ll produce the JSON object for the SVG string and remember to escape quotes properly and keep the structure neat.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}

response.rawBody: {"id":"gen-1772945642-wKnh3ZqdTDyA1PnZrikv","object":"chat.completion","created":1772945642,"model":"openai/gpt-5-mini-2025-08-07","provider":"Azure","system_fingerprint":null,"choices":[{"index":0,"logprobs":null,"finish_reason":"stop","native_finish_reason":"completed","message":{"role":"assistant","content":"{\"guesses\":[\"apple\",\"plate\",\"spoon\",\"fork\",\"fruit\",\"food\",\"cutlery\",\"utensil\",\"dish\",\"meal\",\"dining\",\"dinner\",\"lunch\",\"breakfast\",\"table\",\"bowl\",\"redapple\",\"dessert\",\"healthy\",\"snack\"]}","refusal":null,"reasoning":"**Generating one-word guesses**\n\nI'm focusing on creating a JSON output with an array of 20 one-word guesses, sticking to the EN-US format without spaces or punctuation.
